// SLIDE 01 — HOOK

CHAPTER 8 — DESIGN TOKEN PIPELINES

Before writing any code, the architecture deserves examination, because token pipelines that break do so at predictable

Chapter 8 — Design Token Pipelines — Stage 1 — Declare. The design system declares its token taxonomy in Figma: what collections exist, what modes they conta
NARRATION

Before writing any code, the architecture deserves examination, because token pipelines that break do so at predictable points. Understanding where those points are makes failures legible rather than mysterious. Stage 1 , Declare. The design system declares its token taxonomy in Figma: what collections exist, what modes they contain, what naming conventions apply. This stage happens in the design tool. The machine-readiness contract from Chapter 7 governs it.

// SLIDE 02 — THE ENTERPRISE GATE

THE ENTERPRISE GATE.

The Figma Variables REST API — GET /v1/files/:key/variables/local — requires an Enterprise plan. This is not a soft limi

The Enterprise Gate — A significant portion of design systems engineers working in product companies are on Professional plans. Every token pi
NARRATION

The Figma Variables REST API , GET /v1/files/:key/variables/local , requires an Enterprise plan. This is not a soft limitation or an undocumented quirk. It is an access control decision. On Professional plans or below, the endpoint returns a 403. A significant portion of design systems engineers working in product companies are on Professional plans.

// SLIDE 03 — THE ENTERPRISE PATH: EXTRACT-T

THE ENTERPRISE PATH: EXTRACT-TOKENS.MJS

Returns { resolvedvalue, type: 'COLOR' — 'FLOAT'
if (!cursor — typeof cursor !== 'object') cursor = {};
NARRATION

The extraction script has two jobs: fetch the variable graph from the Figma API, and write it to DTCG-compatible JSON. The intermediate format uses $value, $type, and $description as key names , close to the current DTCG draft rather than conforming to a ratified standard, because the standard has not been ratified. When it is, the extraction script changes and the fixture tests catch the regression. The most consequential function in the script is alias resolution. Figma variables reference each other.

// SLIDE 04 — THE NON-ENTERPRISE PATH: TOKEN

THE NON-ENTERPRISE PATH: TOKENS STUDIO

Variables REST API — GET /v1/files/key/variables/local called by extract-tokens.mjs directly
Tokens Studio — manual — Designer opens Tokens Studio plugin inside Figma and clicks Export; writes JSON to disk or uplo
Tokens Studio — GitHub sync — Tokens Studio configured to push JSON to a GitHub repository branch automatically on each
NARRATION

Tokens Studio (formerly Figma Tokens) is a plugin that runs inside Figma and exports variables to JSON without touching the Variables REST API. Because it runs inside the plugin environment, it has access to local variable data regardless of plan tier. It is the standard non-Enterprise extraction path.

// SLIDE 05 — VALIDATE-TOKENS.MJS

VALIDATE-TOKENS.MJS

Validation sits between extraction and Style Dictionary. Its job is to catch problems that would cause Style Dictionary

validate-tokens.mjs — The validator walks the DTCG JSON tree and checks three things. First, that every token path contains only lowercase slu
NARRATION

Validation sits between extraction and Style Dictionary. Its job is to catch problems that would cause Style Dictionary to either fail visibly or , worse , succeed silently while writing incorrect values. The validator walks the DTCG JSON tree and checks three things. First, that every token path contains only lowercase slugs with no spaces, uppercase letters, or special characters , because Style Dictionary's CSS transform will produce malformed custom property names from anything else.

// SLIDE 06 — STYLE DICTIONARY CONFIGURATION

STYLE DICTIONARY CONFIGURATION.

Style Dictionary reads the DTCG-compatible JSON from tokens/ and emits platform-specific output. The name transformation

Style Dictionary Configuration — sd.config.mjs Usage: npx style-dictionary build --config sd.config.mjs
NARRATION

Style Dictionary reads the DTCG-compatible JSON from tokens/ and emits platform-specific output. The name transformation from slash-separated DTCG paths to kebab-case CSS custom property names is handled by Style Dictionary's built-in CSS transform group. A token at color/brand/primary in the JSON becomes --color-brand-primary in the CSS. sd.config.mjs Usage: npx style-dictionary build --config sd.config.mjs

// SLIDE 07 — .GITHUB/WORKFLOWS/TOKENS.YML

.GITHUB/WORKFLOWS/TOKENS.YML

usesactions/checkout@v4
usesactions/setup-node@v4
runnpm ci
namePreflight check
nameExtract tokens
NARRATION

on: repository_dispatch: types: workflow_dispatch: manual trigger for testing permissions: contents: write pull-requests: write

// SLIDE 08 — FIXTURE TESTS

FIXTURE TESTS

Pipelines are code. Code without tests fails silently in ways that feel like success. The extraction functions are expor

Fixture Tests — test/extract-tokens.test.mjs Usage: node --test test/extract-tokens.test.mjs Requires a saved fixture: test/fixtures/var
NARRATION

Pipelines are code. Code without tests fails silently in ways that feel like success. The extraction functions are exported specifically so they can be tested against a saved API response without making network calls. test/extract-tokens.test.mjs Usage: node --test test/extract-tokens.test.mjs Requires a saved fixture: test/fixtures/variables-response.json

// SLIDE 09 — FAILURE MODES

FAILURE MODES

UID wrench — designer deletes a variable and creates a new one with the same name; the new variable has a different ID —
Sync lag — designer saves without publishing; Variables API returns current save state, not the published library state
Modes explosion — variable collection has many modes (light, dark, high-contrast-light, high-contrast-dark, compact, com
Silent type coercion — Figma FLOAT variables represent spacing, font sizes, line heights, border radii, or durations wit
NARRATION

Several failure modes appear consistently across real-world deployments. The UID wrench. Figma variable IDs are stable across file saves, but if a designer deletes a variable and creates a new one with the same name, the ID changes. The alias chain in other variables that referenced the old ID is now broken. The Chapter 7 preflight catches this, but only if it runs against the current file state , not a cached response from earlier in the same CI run.

// SLIDE 10 — STYLE DICTIONARY AND THE DTCG

STYLE DICTIONARY AND THE DTCG STANDARD

Style Dictionary was created by Danny Banks at Amazon in 2017, initially as an internal tool for managing design tokens

Style Dictionary and the DTCG Standard — The DTCG format — $value, $type, $description — resulted from multi-year work by the Design Tokens Community Group to st
NARRATION

Style Dictionary was created by Danny Banks at Amazon in 2017, initially as an internal tool for managing design tokens across Amazon's product surfaces. It was open-sourced and became the de facto standard for token transformation before the W3C Design Tokens Community Group had published a format specification. The DTCG format , $value, $type, $description , resulted from multi-year work by the Design Tokens Community Group to standardize the JSON structure that tools like Style Dictionary, Tokens Studio, and Theo had been inventing independently.

// SLIDE 11 — WHAT COMES NEXT

WHAT COMES NEXT.

Chapter 9 handles the other half of the canvas: the images, icons, and graphics that live in Figma as vector nodes and n

NARRATION

Chapter 9 handles the other half of the canvas: the images, icons, and graphics that live in Figma as vector nodes and need to arrive in the repository as optimized, production-ready files. Token extraction deals in structured data , names, values, types. Asset extraction deals in rendered output , SVG, PNG, PDF , and introduces a different set of API surfaces and transformation problems.

// SLIDE 12 — THESIS

THE CORE CLAIM.

Chapter 9 handles the other half of the canvas: the images, icons, and graphics that live in Figma as vector nodes and n

NARRATION

Chapter 9 handles the other half of the canvas: the images, icons, and graphics that live in Figma as vector nodes and need to arrive in the repository as optimized, production-ready files. Token extraction deals in structured data , names, values, types. Asset extraction deals in rendered output , SVG, PNG, PDF , and introduces a different set of API surfaces and transformation problems.
